message.content:
TIME-CRITICAL, PARALLEL WORK. Another wheel is building new full-text routes (CORE/OpenAIRE/Zenodo/OAI-PMH) right now. Your job is to do the SLOW NETWORK WORK THAT IS COMMON TO BOTH, so it is already done when those routes land. Do not wait for them.

=== THE SITUATION ===
We hold ~1,500 deduped candidate journal works (DOI + title + venue + year + abstract) in
/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/acquisition_campaign/*.json (frontier_2023_2025_FINAL.json,
oa_cell_discovery.json, canon_and_contrasts_leads.json, plus a citation-graph set).
THEY CARRY NO URLs. Every one needs DOI -> OA-location resolution before any byte can be fetched. That
resolution is pure network latency and it is the long pole. START IT NOW.

Our corpus is TEN journal works. The #1 system has ~98. This is the bottleneck of the whole project.

=== YOUR TASK ===
Build and LAUNCH DETACHED a resumable RESOLVER that, for every candidate DOI:
  1. queries OpenAlex (/works/doi:<doi>) for ALL locations (best_oa_location, locations[], pdf_url,
     landing_page_url, version, license, host)
  2. queries Unpaywall (/v2/<doi>?email=...) for oa_locations
  3. records EVERY discovered location as a DocumentCandidate on the ledger via
     scripts/acquisition.py's Acquirer (record the locator, the host, the version hint, the license
     observation) -- these are OBSERVATIONS, never conclusions
  4. writes a consolidated queue to outputs/acquisition_campaign/resolved_locations.json:
        {doi, title, venue, year, cell_served, locations:[{url, host, version_hint, license, source}]}

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS:
 * LAUNCH IT DETACHED so it survives your turn ending. TWO PREVIOUS FETCHERS DIED SILENTLY AT ~63
   SECONDS because they ran inside an agent turn:
       setsid nohup python <your_script> > outputs/acquisition_campaign/resolve.log 2>&1 &
   Confirm it is alive and the output file is growing, then RETURN. Do NOT block your turn.
 * CHECKPOINT after every DOI (resumable -- on restart, skip DOIs already resolved).
 * BE POLITE. We got IP-throttled all night hammering these APIs. >=1.1s spacing per host, exponential
   backoff on 429, honour Retry-After. OpenAlex and Unpaywall both want a mailto.
 * HONEST OUTCOMES, and this is the whole discipline of this project:
       429 -> THROTTLED (retriable)   401 -> AUTH_FAILED   403 -> ACCESS_DENIED (that URL)
       no OA location found -> NO_OA_LOCATION_KNOWN
   ** A THROTTLE OR AN ERROR MAY NEVER BE RECORDED AS "no OA copy exists". ** That is a fact about our
   request rate, not about the world, and mistaking the two has cost us the entire night.
 * DO NOT FETCH THE DOCUMENTS. Resolution only. Another agent owns fetching. Do NOT touch
   scripts/provenance.py, scripts/event_ledger.py, scripts/source_router.py, scripts/cellcog_composer.py,
   or config/source_routes.yaml -- other agents own those RIGHT NOW.
 * Write ONLY under outputs/acquisition_campaign/. You may READ scripts/acquisition.py to use the Acquirer.

Report: the candidate count, the launch command, PROOF it is running (tail the log, show the output file
growing), and the streaming histogram so far (resolved / no-OA-known / throttled). Do NOT commit.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

326:class Acquirer:
327-    """Emits observations. Cannot emit a status: no method here takes one.
328-
329-    Every method is a thin, boring wrapper over `Ledger.emit`, and that is the point — the interesting
330-    part of this class is what it DOES NOT EXPOSE.
331-    """
332-
333-    def __init__(self, actor: str, ledger: Ledger | None = None, blobs: BlobStore | None = None):
334-        self.actor = actor
335-        self.ledger = ledger if ledger is not None else open_ledger()
336-        self.blobs = blobs if blobs is not None else BlobStore()
337-
338-    # -- 1. ROUTE_PLANNED -------------------------------------------------------------------------
339-    def plan_route(self, unit: str, adapters: Iterable[str], **obs) -> None:
340-        """`route_complete` will mean EVERY ONE OF THESE HAS A TERMINAL OUTCOME RECORD.
341-
342-        It must never mean "an adapter was mapped", so this is called BEFORE the loop, with the
343-        adapters we are ABOUT to try — not after, with the ones that happened to answer.
344-        """
345-        self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED, self.actor,
346-                         adapters=list(adapters), **obs)
347-
348-    # -- 2 & 3. THE EXCEPTION BOUNDARY ------------------------------------------------------------
349-    def get(self, unit: str, adapter: str, url: str, *, tries: int = 4, timeout: int = 30,
350-            candidate_id: str = '', **obs) -> Response:
351-        """One logical request. BACKEND_ATTEMPTED before each attempt; EXACTLY ONE of
352-        RESPONSE_RECEIVED | THROTTLED | BLOCKED at the exception boundary of each attempt.
353-
354-        THE RETRIES SHARE A `request_id`. That is load-bearing: a 429 that a backoff RESOLVED and a
355-        429 that we GAVE UP ON are different facts, and the reducer must be able to tell them apart
356-        without either of them being deleted from the log. Both are here, forever; the reducer reads
357-        the request's FINAL terminal event.
358-
359-          exhausted 429s -> the last terminal event is THROTTLED -> BACKEND_FAILED. FOREVER.
360-          429 then 200   -> the last terminal event is RESPONSE_RECEIVED -> RESPONDED. The 429 is
361-                            still on disk, because the backoff working is not the same as it never
362-                            having been throttled.
363-        """
364-        rid = f'{adapter}#{next(_RID)}'
365-        last: Response | None = None
366-        for attempt in range(1, max(1, tries) + 1):
367-            self._space(url)
368-            self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, self.actor,
369-                             adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, candidate_id=candidate_id, attempt=attempt, **obs)
370-            try:
371-                req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=UA)
372-                # NOTE: `urllib.request.urlopen` is resolved AT CALL TIME, on purpose. The adversary
373-                # harness monkeypatches it to force a 429 storm, and a `from ... import urlopen` would
374-                # bind past the patch and test nothing.
375-                with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as r:
376-                    raw = r.read()
377-                    ctype = r.headers.get('Content-Type', '') or ''
378-                    code = int(getattr(r, 'status', 200) or 200)
379-                self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, self.actor,
380-                                 adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, candidate_id=candidate_id, attempt=attempt,
381-                                 http_status=code, content_type=ctype, n_bytes=len(raw))
382-                return Response(RESPONDED, code, raw, ctype, url, adapter, rid)
383-
384-            except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
385-                code = int(getattr(e, 'code', 0) or 0)
386-
===DC===
279:class DocumentCandidate:
280-    """A URL AN ADAPTER PROPOSED. NOT a document, not a version, not evidence — a lead with a lineage.
281-
282-    `version_hint` and `license_observation` are OBSERVATIONS ONLY, and the suffixes are load-bearing:
283-    `acceptedVersion` from a repository is a string a repository emitted, and the entire V9 P0 was the

def open_ledger(path: Path | str | None = None) -> Ledger:
    """THE ONE DURABLE LEDGER, opened AS IT STANDS ON DISK. Every fetcher in a run shares it.

    `Ledger.load` re-reads the history, so a fetcher started after another fetcher finished REDUCES
    OVER WHAT HAPPENED, not over the handful of events it emitted itself.
    """
    return Ledger.load(Path(path) if path else LEDGER_PATH)


def content_host(url: str) -> str:
    """The adapter name for a DOCUMENT fetch. A content host is a backend too: if nber.org 403s us,
    we were BLOCKED BY NBER, and that is a fact worth keeping — distinct from "no copy exists"."""
    try:
        return 'content:' + (urllib.parse.urlparse(url).netloc or 'unknown')
    except Exception:
        return 'content:unknown'

215-            bad.append(f'key {k!r} IS a label — the reducer derives it; a component may not set it')
216-            continue

223-def _observations(events: list['Event']) -> list['Event']:
224-    """THE OBSERVATIONS ONLY. A reducer NEVER reads another reducer's recorded verdict.
225-
226-    `record_derivation()` writes audit artifacts into the same log, and the module docstring has
227-    always claimed "NO REDUCER READS THEM". That was true only BY ACCIDENT OF KIND CHOICE — nothing
228-    enforced it. It was one line from being false in the worst way: a derived record written with
229-    kind=CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED is picked up by `derive_content_profile`'s `for e in events` loop,
230-    which keeps the LAST match — so the reducer would have read ITS OWN PREVIOUS OUTPUT as if it were
231-    an observation of the bytes, and the label would have become a cache of itself. That is the cookie
232-    banner, again, from the inside.
233-
234-    So the invariant is now structural, in one place, and every derive_*() below goes through it.
235-    """
236-    return [e for e in events if 'derived_by' not in e.payload]

#:
#: Sol V9 §1, verbatim: "The adapter must not write FULLTEXT, THIS_WORK, VERSION_OF_RECORD, ADMISSIBLE,
#: or an expression edge. Those are reducer outputs derived from immutable metadata and document bytes."
#: FULLTEXT, ADMISSIBLE and `same work` were already here. THIS_WORK and VERSION_OF_RECORD were not —
#: and an adapter that may write `version_of_record` into a payload has been handed, by the back door,
#: precisely the authority the V9 P0 was about taking away from it. A repository saying `acceptedVersion`
#: is an OBSERVATION; a component writing `version_of_record` is a VERDICT.
RESERVED_VALUES = (
    'fulltext', 'full text', 'full-text',
    'complete', 'route_complete',
    'no evidence', 'no free copy', 'no free text', 'not available', 'nothing found',
    'same work', 'same_work', 'this work', 'this_work', 'different work', 'different_work',
    'version equivalent', 'version of record', 'version_of_record',
    'high quality', 'high_quality', 'low quality', 'low_quality',
    'paywalled', 'still paywalled',
    'supported', 'searched_none', 'conflicted',
    'admissible', 'inadmissible', 'eligible',
    'verified', 'span-verified', 'fabrication-proof',
)

#: Keys that ARE labels. Naming one is an attempt to set the label directly.
RESERVED_KEYS = (
    'content_status', 'status', 'label', 'verdict', 'quality', 'tier', 'grade',
    'fulltext_source', 'is_fulltext', 'route_complete', 'complete', 'coverage',
    'coverage_status', 'eligibility', 'binding', 'admissible', 'evidence_status',
)

#: Fields that are RAW TEXT QUOTED FROM THE WORLD. A PDF may contain any word at all; that is not
#: the component asserting it. These are exempt from the value scan (never from the key scan).
#: NOTE what is NOT here: `note`. A free-text `note` is the COMPONENT talking, not the world, and it
#: is precisely where a conclusion would be smuggled in ("note: no free copy exists"). It stays
#: scanned. (The guard caught exactly that in this file's own replay code, and the fix was to reword
#: the note into an observation — not to exempt the field.)
#: `locator` is here for the same reason `url` is: IT IS A URL. Publishers put our own reserved words

#: guard that refused to record THE ADDRESS THE BYTES CAME FROM — because the address contains the

#: bibliography. There is a journal called `Information & Management` and there could be one called
#: `Complete Systems`; a guard that refused to record which journal we asked about, because the

content.text:
The resolver is live and producing. Summary:

## Candidate count
**500 unique DOIs** currently on disk in `outputs/acquisition_campaign/` (deduped across `frontier_2023_2025_FINAL.json` + its run1/records twins → 298 works, `oa_cell_discovery.json` → 202 works incl. its `discovery_leads_nonjournal`). `canon_and_contrasts_leads.json` and the citation-graph set **are not on disk yet** — so the resolver **re-globs the whole campaign dir on every pass** and will pick them up automatically without a restart when the other wheel drops them (it polls for up to 20 idle minutes after the queue drains).

## Launch (detached, survives my turn)
```
cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel && setsid nohup python3 outputs/acquisition_campaign/resolve_locations.py \
  > outputs/acquisition_campaign/resolve.log 2>&1 &
```
PID **167952**, confirmed alive after launch, detached from the agent's process group.

## Proof it is running
```
[start] 05:41:58 checkpoint carried 0 DOIs
[pass] 500 candidates known, 500 to work
[05:42:09] 10/500 | {'RESOLVED': 10} | spacing=1.2s
[05:42:21] 20/500 | {'RESOLVED': 20} | spacing=1.2s
[05:42:33] 30/500 | {'RESOLVED': 29, 'NO_OA_LOCATION_KNOWN': 1} | spacing=1.2s
```
`resolver_checkpoint.jsonl` 38 rows and growing; `resolved_locations.json` rewritten atomically (38 KB and growing); **345 events already on the shared ledger** under actor `resolve_locations`, e.g. a real `candidate_identified`:
```
{"adapter":"unpaywall","candidate_id":"cand:93448c236cf3ab40","host":"pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov",
 "host_type":"repository","license_observation":"cc-by","oa_version":"submittedVersion",
 "proposed_by":"unpaywall:url","url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC13318031/"}
```

## Streaming histogram (first 38 DOIs)
| outcome | n |
|---|---|
| RESOLVED | 37 |
| NO_OA_LOCATION_KNOWN | 1 |
| THROTTLED | 0 |
| AUTH_FAILED / ACCESS_DENIED | 0 |

**79 locations** discovered so far (~2.1 per work). Backend outcomes: openalex 200×37 / 404×1, unpaywall 200×37 / 404×1 — the single NO_OA is honest (both indexes *answered* 404; nobody was throttled). Rate ~1.2 s/DOI ⇒ the 500 finish in ~10 min; the file keeps growing after that as new candidate files land.

## Honesty discipline enforced in code
- `RESOLVED` only if a backend named a location; `NO_OA_LOCATION_KNOWN` **only when both backends answered (200 or 404) and neither named one**. A 429 outranks absence in the decision order, so a throttle can never be written down as "no OA copy exists" — it stays `THROTTLED` and **retriable** (up to 3 passes; if it never clears it stays THROTTLED forever, never absence). 401 → `AUTH_FAILED`, 403 → `ACCESS_DENIED` (that URL only), timeouts/5xx → `TRANSPORT_ERROR` (retriable).
- On any 429 the run backs off globally (spacing ×1.5, capped 20 s, plus a 60 s sleep), on top of `Acquirer`'s existing per-host 1.2 s spacing and exponential retry.
- Checkpoint fsync'd after **every** DOI; restart skips terminal DOIs and re-tries retriable ones.
- No documents fetched. Nothing written outside `outputs/acquisition_campaign/` except append-only events on the shared ledger via `Acquirer` (the sanctioned path). `provenance.py`, `event_ledger.py`, `source_router.py`, `cellcog_composer.py`, `config/source_routes.yaml` untouched. Nothing committed.
